gaps.title: Workflow State Persistence Failure During Concurrent Execution
gaps.breaks: Race condition when multiple workflows for same tenant execute simultaneously, causing state corruption
gaps.scenario: Two concurrent workflows for tenant_id=42 both try to update the same state variable, leading to lost updates and inconsistent workflow progression
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent execution of multiple LangGraph workflows for the same tenant. Create 5 parallel workflows that all modify the same state variable. Verify that state updates are atomic and no data is lost. Test with different workflow variants (Mini, PARWA, High) to ensure race conditions don't occur at any tier.

gaps.title: Conditional Branching Logic Flaw in Variant Graphs
gaps.breaks: Incorrect routing between Mini, PARWA, and High tier workflows due to misconfigured conditional logic
gaps.scenario: A PARWA tenant with a complex query gets routed to the simple response path instead of the approval path, causing incorrect handling of sensitive customer data
gaps.prompt: Create a comprehensive test suite that validates all conditional branching paths in the Variant-Aware Pipeline Routing system. Test each variant (Mini, PARWA, High) with different input types (simple, complex, sensitive) and verify that each correctly follows its designated graph structure. Include edge cases where input characteristics are ambiguous and test fallback behavior.

gaps.title: Human Checkpoint Timeout Without Fallback
gaps.breaks: Workflows hang indefinitely when human approval times out, with no automatic fallback or escalation
gaps.scenario: A High tier workflow requires human approval for a complex query, but the approver is unavailable. The workflow state remains in "pending_approval" forever, blocking all subsequent tickets for that tenant
gaps.prompt: Write tests that simulate human checkpoint timeout scenarios. Verify that workflows properly time out after configured thresholds and implement appropriate fallback behaviors (escalation, auto-approval for low-risk cases, or graceful degradation). Test different timeout configurations and ensure state is properly cleaned up after timeout.

gaps.title: Context Compression Quality Degradation at Critical Thresholds
gaps.breaks: Over-aggressive compression at 80% token limit loses critical information, causing responses to be irrelevant
gaps.scenario: A conversation reaches 80% of context window, triggering compression that removes key customer details from earlier messages, resulting in an AI response that doesn't address the customer's specific needs
gaps.prompt: Develop tests that validate context compression quality at various thresholds (60%, 80%, 95%). Include test cases with different conversation types (technical support, billing inquiries, emotional support) and verify that critical information is preserved. Test all compression strategies (summarization, truncation, extractive) and measure impact on response relevance.

gaps.title: Context Health Meter False Positives/Negatives
gaps.breaks: Health meter incorrectly signals problems or misses actual issues, leading to unnecessary alerts or silent failures
gaps.scenario: The Context Health Meter reports "critical" status for a healthy conversation due to temporary network latency, causing unnecessary workflow interruptions. Conversely, it fails to detect when compression has made context unusable
gaps.prompt: Create tests that validate the Context Health Meter's accuracy. Include test cases with: 1) healthy conversations that should remain "healthy", 2) conversations with gradual degradation that should properly transition through warning to critical, 3) conversations with temporary issues that should recover, and 4) edge cases where metrics conflict (e.g., high relevance but old context).

gaps.title: Tenant Isolation Break in Workflow Graph Sharing
gaps.breaks: Variant-specific workflow configurations leak between tenants, causing incorrect processing
gaps.scenario: A High tier tenant's workflow configuration (with human checkpoints) gets applied to a Mini tier tenant after a cache refresh, causing unnecessary processing delays and approval steps
gaps.prompt: Write tests that verify tenant isolation in the Variant-Aware Pipeline Routing system. Test scenarios including: 1) concurrent requests from different tenant tiers, 2) cache invalidation events that might cause configuration mixing, 3) shared resource access patterns that could lead to data leaks. Ensure each tenant always receives the correct variant-specific graph configuration.

gaps.title: Missing Rollback on Workflow State Transition Failure
gaps.breaks: Partial state updates during workflow transitions leave system in inconsistent state when failures occur
gaps.scenario: During a workflow transition from "processing" to "completed", the system updates the workflow status but fails to record the final response. The workflow appears complete but no response was actually generated
gaps.prompt: Develop tests that validate workflow state transition atomicity. For each workflow type and variant, simulate failures at different points in state transitions and verify that: 1) the system properly rolls back to previous state on failure, 2) no partial state updates remain, and 3) appropriate error logging occurs. Test both database-level and Redis-level state persistence.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 7

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Workflow State Persistence Failure During Concurrent Execution
What breaks: Race condition when multiple workflows for same tenant execute simultaneously, causing state corruption
Real scenario: Two concurrent workflows for tenant_id=42 both try to update the same state variable, leading to lost updates and inconsistent workflow progression
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent execution of multiple LangGraph workflows for the same tenant. Create 5 parallel workflows that all modify the same state variable. Verify that state updates are atomic and no data is lost. Test with different workflow variants (Mini, PARWA, High) to ensure race conditions don't occur at any tier.

Title: Conditional Branching Logic Flaw in Variant Graphs

Include edge cases where input characteristics are ambiguous and test fallback behavior.
